title: Grassidium
description: Makes grass alive! Adds the ability for grass, flowers, and ferns to spread and grow based on environmental conditions.
body:
Grassidium makes grass alive! It adds the ability for grass, flowers, and ferns to spread and grow based on environmental conditions. Your world's static plants become a dynamic, spreading ecosystem that reacts to light, rain, and plant density.

## ✨ Features

| Feature                      | Description                                                                                                                           |
|:-----------------------------|:--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **🌿 Dynamic Spreading**     | Plants won't stay put! Grass, flowers, and ferns slowly spread to nearby empty dirt blocks.                                           |
| **🌱 Tall Growth**           | Small plants like grass and ferns can grow into their tall versions.                                                                  |
| **☀️ Environmental Factors** | Plant growth isn't random. It's affected by **light levels** (each plant has its preferred light) and grows faster in **rain**.       |
| **👨‍🌾 Crowding**           | Plants won't spread if the area is too crowded with their own kind.                                                                   |
| **🌻 Plant Species**         | The mod knows the difference between species (grass, fern, flower) for crowding rules.                                                |
| **🚧 Obstacle Awareness**    | Plants check their path before spreading. Solid blocks stop growth, while fences or glass panes only lower the chance of spreading.   |
| **⚙️ In-game Config**        | Change mod settings directly in the game (Mods -> Grassidium -> Config). Adjust spread chances, growth rates, rain effects, and more! |

## ⚙️ Configuration

You can change almost everything directly in the game! Go to **Mods -> Grassidium -> Config**. Or edit `grassidium-common.toml`.

Don't like a feature? You can disable spreading entirely, or fine-tune values to fit your game.
